subject: Careers
topic: Career pathways presentation and station challenge

teacher_guide.big_idea: Students can build career awareness by listening to classmates, rotating through short challenges, and giving structured feedback.
learning_goals: I can listen for useful career details in a presentation.
I can use station clues to identify or compare career options.
I can give respectful feedback that matches my ratings.

timing.activity: Set the challenge and review rotation routines.
Short presentation share and peer rubric notes.
Debrief patterns and complete exit ticket.

teacher_notes: Keep the rubric focused on listening quality and respectful feedback, not polished speaking alone.
Use the answer key as a quick reference during the station rotation instead of stopping the flow for every close answer.

content.subtitle: Listen, rotate, solve, and give useful feedback.

rows: Use the peer-review rubric while you listen to one presentation.
Move through the station cards and record strong answers in your organizer.
Use the exit ticket to name one career clue pattern you noticed.

rubric_sheet.title: Career Presentation Peer Review
rubric_sheet.purpose_line: Use fair, respectful, and specific feedback.

criteria.name: Clear speaking
criteria.descriptor: The presenter spoke clearly enough for the audience to understand.

criteria.name: Organization
criteria.descriptor: The presentation was organized and easy to follow.

criteria.name: Useful details
criteria.descriptor: Specific details helped the audience learn about the career.

success_criteria: I gave fair and respectful feedback.

station_cards.title: Outdoor Career Challenge Stations
instructions: Work in pairs or groups of 3.
Complete as many stations as you can.

cards.title: Guess the career
cards.prompt: I work with patients. I need strong communication skills. I may work in a hospital or clinic.

cards.title: Name the strength match
cards.prompt: This job often needs patience, close attention, and steady hands.
cards.task: Write one career that matches those strengths.
cards.teacher_note: Good chance to talk about more than one acceptable answer.

cards.title: Where might they work?
cards.prompt: This person may work outside, measure land, and help plan what gets built.
cards.teacher_note: Push students to use the clue, not just guess a building job.

cards.title: Follow the clues
cards.prompt: This person explains ideas clearly and helps others learn new skills.

graphic_organizer.title: Career Challenge Organizer
graphic_organizer.prompt: Use this organizer to keep the strongest answer and the clue that helped your team decide.
success_criteria: I recorded the best answer and the clue that helped us decide.

answer_key.title: Career Challenge Answer Key

entries.teacher_note: Accept equivalent nursing roles only if the clues still fit.
Accept hygienist if students justify the close-attention clue.
Do not accept architect here; the outside measurement clue matters.
Accept tutor only if students explain the teaching clue clearly.

scoring_guidance: Use teacher notes when a student gives a close but not exact answer.

exit_ticket.title: Exit Ticket
exit_ticket.prompt: What is one clue pattern you noticed across the station cards, and how did it help your team decide?
success_criteria: I named one pattern and explained how it helped.
